clauses.text: Acquirer hereby agrees to purchase from Seller, and Seller agrees to sell to Acquirer, all of the issued and outstanding shares of the Company for a purchase price of $15,000,000, subject to adjustment as set forth herein.

Seller shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Acquirer from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, and expenses arising from or related to any breach of Seller's representations and warranties or any fraud by Seller.

IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, EXCEPT FOR BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS OR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS.
clauses.difficulty_note: Broad exclusion but carves out indemnities; should add cap

clauses.text: Seller represents and warrants that: (a) it has full power and authority to enter into this Agreement, (b) the execution and delivery of this Agreement does not conflict with any agreement to which Seller is a party, (c) there are no pending or threatened claims that could materially affect the Company, and (d) all financial statements provided to Acquirer are accurate in all material respects.
clauses.difficulty_note: Representations are broad and should be more specific

clauses.text: The closing shall occur within ten business days after the satisfaction or waiver of all conditions to closing, or at such other time as the parties may mutually agree in writing.

Acquirer shall maintain commercially reasonable insurance coverage for a period of three years following the closing, including directors and officers liability insurance with limits of not less than $5,000,000.
clauses.difficulty_note: Commercially reasonable is vague; D&O coverage should be specified

clauses.text: For a period of five years following the closing, Seller shall not directly or indirectly (a) own, manage, operate, or control any business that competes with the Company, (b) solicit any employee of the Company to leave their employment, or (c) solicit any customer of the Company to cease doing business with the Company.
clauses.difficulty_note: Non-compete is extremely broad; five years is excessive

clauses.text: All confidential information of the Company shall be deemed Confidential Information of Acquirer following the closing, and Seller shall protect such information using at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.

This Agreement, together with all exhibits and schedules attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings. No amendment shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.

Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved by binding arbitration in Wilmington, Delaware administered by the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator shall have the power to award equitable relief. Judgment on the award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction.
clauses.difficulty_note: Arbitration location may favor one party

clauses.text: Seller warrants that the software products owned by the Company do not infringe any third party intellectual property rights and that to Seller's knowledge, no third party is infringing on the Company's intellectual property.
clauses.difficulty_note: Knowledge qualifier weakens warranty; should have clearer scope

clauses.text: All intellectual property created by Company employees prior to closing shall remain the exclusive property of the Company. All intellectual property created by Seller in connection with the Company shall be assigned to the Company prior to closing.
